termux_from_fdroid.noob:
If Termux is behaving strangely, it might be the Play Store version — which is no longer maintained.

  Solution: Install Termux from F-Droid instead:
  1. Go to f-droid.org on your phone
  2. Download and install the F-Droid app
  3. Search for Termux and install it

  The F-Droid version gets regular updates and works properly.
termux_from_fdroid.learner:
Termux on Play Store hasn't been updated since 2020 and is broken.
  Install from F-Droid: https://f-droid.org/en/packages/com.termux/
  Or download the APK directly from GitHub releases.
termux_from_fdroid.pro: Install from F-Droid or github.com/termux/termux-app/releases. Play Store version is dead.

x11_gui_setup.noob:
To run apps with windows and buttons in Termux, you need Termux:X11.

  Steps:
  1. Install Termux:X11 app from F-Droid
  2. In Termux:
  $ pkg install x11-repo
  $ pkg install termux-x11-nightly xfce4
  3. Start it: $ termux-x11 :1 &
  4. Open Termux:X11 app on your phone
x11_gui_setup.learner:
Termux:X11 provides an X11 display server for Termux.
  $ pkg install x11-repo
  $ pkg install termux-x11-nightly
  Then start X: termux-x11 :1 &
  Set display: export DISPLAY=:1
  Now GUI apps work.
x11_gui_setup.pro: pkg install x11-repo termux-x11-nightly. termux-x11 :1 &. export DISPLAY=:1.

port_forwarding_adb.noob:
You can connect to Termux from your computer over WiFi:

  On your phone in Termux:
  $ pkg install openssh
  $ sshd
  $ whoami  (note your username)
  $ ip addr show  (note your IP address)

  On your computer:
  $ ssh username@phone_ip_address -p 8022
port_forwarding_adb.learner:
Termux SSH server runs on port 8022 (not 22).
  $ pkg install openssh && sshd  # start server
  $ ssh-keygen  # create key pair for auth
  Connect from PC: ssh user@phone_ip -p 8022
port_forwarding_adb.pro: sshd on port 8022. ssh user@phone_ip -p 8022. Or adb forward tcp:8022 tcp:8022.
